headline: Venezuelans call for help to rescue survivors from rubble after twin earthquakes
description: International aid has only just begun to arrive, while local residents have been trying to clear the rubble with their bare hands since Wednesday. As the hours go by, the death toll of 920, along with 50,000 missing, is likely to rise.

headline: One year after replacing Wagner, Russia's Africa Corps struggles in Mali
Lebanon and Israel sign framework agreement in Washington for 'lasting peace and security'
Heatwave fuels frustration in Paris's northern suburbs: 'If they'd just given us shutters, we wouldn't be suffering like this'
description: Over the past week, daily life in two large concrete housing complexes in Seine-Saint-Denis has changed dramatically due to extreme heat. Residents share their struggles and anger as they try to help each other cope.

headline: Parents outraged by lack of school guidelines during heatwave: 'We've had to scramble to make childcare arrangements'
description: As classroom conditions worsen, parents have tried to adapt by working from home, taking time off or relying on other adults for help. All have criticized the authorities' lack of preparation and have expressed concern for the years ahead.

headline: Mansplashing: Why do men splash everyone at the pool?
description: The behavior of the so-called 'pool man,' who thrashes around in the water and sprawls out across swimming lanes, highlights the power dynamics at play in the shared social space of the public swimming pool.

headline: Dembélé shines at World Cup, silencing critics
description: After scoring a hat trick and named man of the match as France defeated Norway 4-1 on Friday, Ousmane Dembélé is making an impact at the 2026 World Cup. The long-awaited breakthrough with the French national team has finally arrived.

Paris Pride march postponed over heatwave
Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
description: 'Donbas: The War of Attrition' (1/2). Just a few kilometers from one of Ukraine's fiercest fronts, these two cities are under drone strikes that make residents' lives very difficult. Both civilians and soldiers fear the bombings will intensify.
byline: Thomas d’Istria and  Virginie NGuyen Hoang

headline: How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
description: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

headline: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
description: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.
